seoTitle: How to Use Multiple Boards in One Jira Project
metaDescription: Learn how to create multiple Scrum and Kanban boards within a single Jira project using filters and custom settings for each team.
intro: Different teams often need different views of the same project without spinning up entirely separate Jira projects. This guide covers creating multiple boards for a single Jira project, configuring filters for each, and customizing them independently.

steps.heading: Understand why you'd use multiple boards
steps.body: Multiple boards let different teams — like development, QA, or support — track their own workflow within the same underlying project.

steps.heading: Create a new board
steps.body: Set up a new board and choose whether it should be Scrum or Kanban based on how that team works.

steps.heading: Configure board filters
steps.body: Set a filter so the new board only shows the issues relevant to that specific team or workflow.

steps.heading: Customize board settings
steps.body: Adjust columns, swimlanes, and other settings independently for each board so it fits how that team actually works.

faq.question: Can I have more than one board in a single Jira project?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create multiple Scrum and Kanban boards that all pull from the same project using different filters.

faq.question: How do I control what issues show up on each board?
faq.answer: Configure a board filter for each board so it only displays the issues relevant to that specific team or workflow.

faq.question: Can each board have different column and swimlane settings?
faq.answer: Yes, each board can be customized independently, even if they're all pulling from the same underlying project.

faq.question: Why use multiple boards instead of multiple projects?
faq.answer: Multiple boards let separate teams track their own work without fragmenting your data across different Jira projects.
